skill.name: scvi-tools
skill.description: Guides advanced single-cell omics analysis with scvi-tools, including scVI batch correction, totalVI and MultiVI integration, differential expression, spatial methods, and model selection.
skill.summary: Use scvi-tools deep generative models for single-cell omics analysis.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and URL patterns, but reviewed examples show Markdown code fences, mathematical notation, and documentation links. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious execution intent was found. The residual risk is low because the skill contains benign install commands and links to external documentation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives. The cited content is Markdown formatting, Python scvi-tools examples, or benign installation commands, with no dynamic command execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations show fenced Markdown and Python examples rather than Ruby backticks or shell execution. The only shell commands are documented package installation commands.

low_findings.title: Scientific Notation Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static weak-cryptography findings are false positives. The cited material discusses Bayesian probability, variational inference, and count distributions, not cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain formulas such as posterior distributions, ELBO, and negative binomial models. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage was found in this context.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill includes hardcoded links to official scvi-tools documentation and examples. These links do not transmit secrets or user data, but users should still treat external documentation as outside marketplace control.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed URLs point to docs.scvi-tools.org documentation pages. No network request code, tracking endpoint, or exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static system reconnaissance findings are false positives. The reviewed line recommends saving trained models and using GPU acceleration for scientific workflows, not collecting host information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited SKILL.md section is a best-practices list for model training. It does not instruct the assistant to enumerate files, users, networks, or environment secrets.

content.value_statement: Single-cell omics projects need careful model choice and reproducible workflows. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code apply scvi-tools for probabilistic integration, annotation, and differential analysis.
actual_capabilities: Select scvi-tools models for RNA, ATAC, multimodal, spatial, and specialized single-cell data.
Explain scVI, scANVI, totalVI, MultiVI, PeakVI, DestVI, and related model workflows.
Guide AnnData setup with count layers, batch keys, labels, covariates, and model outputs.
Support probabilistic differential expression planning and result interpretation.
Recommend practical training settings, GPU usage, model saving, and downstream Scanpy integration.
Clarify theoretical concepts such as variational inference, ELBO, latent spaces, and count likelihoods.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a bundled scvi-tools runtime or dataset.
Large model training still depends on local compute, memory, and GPU availability.
Model results require biological validation and careful review of input annotations.
It does not replace the official scvi-tools documentation for version-specific API changes.

use_cases.title: Integrate scRNA-seq batches
use_cases.description: Plan an scVI workflow that registers batch covariates, trains a model, and exports latent representations for clustering.

use_cases.title: Analyze multimodal experiments
use_cases.description: Choose totalVI or MultiVI for CITE-seq and multiome datasets, then interpret joint RNA, protein, and accessibility outputs.

use_cases.title: Standardize advanced workflows
use_cases.description: Create reusable guidance for differential expression, annotation transfer, spatial deconvolution, and model persistence.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the right scvi-tools model for my single-cell dataset. I have RNA counts, batch labels, and cell type labels.

prompt_templates.title: Build an scVI Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a reproducible scVI workflow for AnnData input with raw counts, batch correction, latent embedding, UMAP, and model saving.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Differential Expression
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a scvi-tools differential expression analysis between two cell groups, including effect size thresholds and result interpretation.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Multimodal Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare totalVI and MultiVI for my multimodal dataset, then propose setup choices, covariates, validation checks, and downstream outputs.

output_examples.input: I have three scRNA-seq batches and need integration guidance.
output: Recommended model: scVI for unsupervised batch-aware integration.
Input check: use raw counts in AnnData and keep batch labels in observations.
Workflow outline: register data, train scVI, inspect latent space, then run neighbors and UMAP.
Validation: compare biological labels and batch labels after integration.

output_examples.input: Should I use totalVI or MultiVI for CITE-seq data?
output: Use totalVI when each cell has paired RNA and protein measurements.
Use MultiVI when combining paired or unpaired RNA and accessibility data.
Check modality availability, feature naming, batch covariates, and missing data patterns before setup.

output_examples.input: How should I interpret scvi-tools differential expression output?
output: Review effect size, uncertainty, Bayes factors, and false discovery control together.
Avoid ranking genes by one metric without checking group definitions and model assumptions.
Validate important markers with domain knowledge and independent quality checks.

best_practices: Use raw count data and keep normalized values in separate AnnData layers.
Register known technical covariates before training models.
Save trained models and record package versions for reproducible analysis.
anti_patterns: Do not train scvi-tools models on log-normalized expression as the primary input.
Do not ignore batch, donor, protocol, or chemistry labels when they affect the experiment.
Do not treat latent embeddings or differential results as final biological proof without validation.

faq.question: When should I use scvi-tools instead of Scanpy alone?
faq.answer: Use scvi-tools when probabilistic modeling, batch correction, multimodal integration, or uncertainty-aware differential analysis is central to the project.

faq.question: What input format does this skill expect?
faq.answer: It assumes AnnData objects with raw count data, feature metadata, cell metadata, and relevant covariates.

faq.question: Does the skill run model training for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and examples. Training depends on your local Python environment and compute resources.

faq.question: Which models are covered?
faq.answer: The references cover scVI, scANVI, AUTOZI, VeloVI, PeakVI, totalVI, MultiVI, DestVI, Tangram, Solo, and related models.

faq.question: Do I need a GPU?
faq.answer: A GPU is not mandatory for small data, but it is strongly recommended for large datasets and repeated training.

faq.question: Can this help with differential expression?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains scvi-tools differential expression workflows, effect size choices, uncertainty metrics, and interpretation limits.
